configurePresets.name: dev
configurePresets.description: Development build: Debug, warnings enabled, no sanitizers

configurePresets.name: release
configurePresets.description: Release build: Optimized, warnings-as-errors, LTO enabled

configurePresets.name: vcpkg
configurePresets.description: Adds vcpkg toolchain (set VCPKG_ROOT environment variable)

configurePresets.name: dev-vcpkg
configurePresets.description: Development build with vcpkg dependency management

configurePresets.name: release-vcpkg
configurePresets.description: Release build with vcpkg dependency management

configurePresets.name: llm-vcpkg
configurePresets.description: AI-assisted dev preset (CLAUDE.md): warnings-as-errors plus AddressSanitizer + UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer. Sanitizers are applied via global flags (not the ENABLE_SANITIZER_* cache vars) so the plain-add_executable test targets are instrumented too. Run clang-format/clang-tidy/cppcheck via ./fix_and_format.sh and the gated test+coverage flow via ./run_test.sh.

configurePresets.name: ci-coverage
configurePresets.description: CI preset: Debug + gcov coverage instrumentation, no sanitizers. Used by .github/workflows/ci.yml; the gcov flags mirror run_test.sh (-fprofile-update=atomic keeps the multi-threaded SchedulerTests' counters correct).

configurePresets.name: tsan-vcpkg
configurePresets.description: ThreadSanitizer build for the Vulkan-free reactive core (ThreadPoolScheduler). Used by the CI 'tsan' lane, which builds + runs only the CPU-only core tests (SchedulerTests, GraphTests, TemporalTests) — running TSan over the GPU suite only surfaces noise from the uninstrumented Mesa driver. TSan is mutually exclusive with ASan/UBSan, hence a separate preset from llm-vcpkg.
